description: Draws a two-branch hyperbola on a 2D graph from a center point and transverse/conjugate semi-axes, or from a standard-form equation string. Center takes a point() expression or variable. Rotation uses the rotation() expression.
utility: Draw a hyperbola on a 2D graph by center+axes or equation

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to draw on

arguments.name: center
arguments.description: Center of the hyperbola as a point() expression or point variable. Defaults to origin if omitted

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: Semi-transverse axis length (along rotation direction, where vertices are). Must be positive

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: Semi-conjugate axis length (perpendicular). Must be positive

arguments.name: rotation
arguments.description: Rotation angle using rotation() expression - e.g., rotation(pi/4). Default is 0 (horizontal transverse axis)

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Alternative to parametric args - standard form equation string e.g. "x^2/16 - y^2/9 = 1"

alternates.syntax: hyperbola(G, point(G, h, k), a, b)
alternates.description: Hyperbola at a point with transverse axis a and conjugate axis b
Hyperbola centered at origin with semi-axes a and b

alternates.syntax: hyperbola(G, point(G, h, k), a, b, rotation(angle))

examples.scenario: Vertical hyperbola from equation in a geometry graph with labeled vertices and foci
Hyperbola at origin with semi-axes 3 and 2
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
H = hyperbola(G, point(G, 0, 0), 3, 2)

G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
H = hyperbola(G, "x^2/16 - y^2/9 = 1")

G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
H = hyperbola(G, point(G, 0, 0), 3, 2, rotation(pi/4))
